extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR Tadej won the uphill sprint in Longwy, taking yellow jersey via time bonuses after UAE controlled final kilometers
tactical_patterns: UAE Team Emirates drilled final kilometers before Cat-3 finish climb despite having no yellow, signaling aggressive bonus-second hunting strategy.
Van Aert's yellow jersey defense collapsed when Jumbo-Visma failed to chase intermediate breakaway time gaps on Stage 6.
MATTHEWS Michael positioned fourth wheel in final meters but lacked speed to contest Pogačar's accelerations on 12% kicker.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: Lost yellow by 2:54 after team abandoned defense, suggesting Jumbo prioritized Vingegaard GC over Van Aert stage ambitions
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Took yellow earliest in career via bonus seconds rather than time trial, revealing willingness to race aggressively from Week 1
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: neutral: Finished 13 seconds down after leading into finale, confirming EF lacks firepower to hold off UAE's organized lead-out
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Tom: positive: First British rider in Tour top-5 at this stage, moved to fifth despite early crash affecting Ineos
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: Positioned perfectly fourth wheel but couldn't match Pogačar's acceleration on steep gradient, exposing pure climbing limitation
